provider: HKUST 2025/26 BBA in General Business Management Major and School Requirements
note: This supplement is cohort-specific to students admitted in 2025-26. The official Major Requirements PDF defines General Business Management as any 9 courses offered by departments under the School of Business and Management, totaling at least 29 credits, with at least 4 courses at 3000-level or above. The supplement records all 194 undergraduate course rows in the official 2025-26 ACCT, ECON, FINA, ISOM, MARK and MGMT catalogs as a dated snapshot, then keeps the 16 codes used by the School Requirements only in their non-reusable School role; the remaining 178 rows form the visible Major-elective candidate snapshot. This snapshot is not a permanent closed graduation pool: eligibility remains subject to prerequisites, exclusions, course offering, approvals and the rule that courses counted toward School Requirements may not also fulfill the Major unless the Dean or designate approves. The School Requirements preserve all official alternatives and total 39-40 credits for the example GBM pathway. Variable-credit courses retain their official ranges and use local credits 0 so unknown attained credits are not counted as zero. Recommended Year and semester are assigned only to code-specific School courses explicitly placed in the official 2025-26 normative pathway; its anonymous GBM elective blocks of 8, 7, 8 and 6 credits across Year 3 Fall through Year 4 Spring are not assigned to individual catalog courses. University Common Core, Legal Education and additional approved courses needed to reach 120 credits remain outside this Programme-local supplement, so these rows must not be treated as a graduation-completion decision.
